title: Three bugs caught by adversarial review

implementer.name: claude
implementer.context: the issue, repository, tests, and its own reasoning

reviewer.name: claude
reviewer.context: only the diff, told to assume the code is wrong

cases.title: The async close
cases.summary: Reviewer catches a failure-path cleanup bug.

events.title: Task received
events.summary: Fix concurrent session refresh without changing the public API.

blocks.content: Users are occasionally signed out when two requests refresh an expired session at the same time. I’ll trace the refresh path and keep the public API unchanged.

blocks.title: issue #1842
blocks.content: investigating session refresh race

events.title: Plan the fix
events.summary: Deduplicate callers around one in-flight promise per session.

blocks.content: I’ll keep one in-flight refresh promise per session. Concurrent callers reuse it; the entry is cleared when the request settles.

blocks.title: Read
blocks.content: src/auth/session.ts · tests/auth/session.test.ts

blocks.title: Search results
blocks.content: refreshSession has 4 callers; all depend on the current Promise<Session> contract.

events.title: Patch prepared
events.summary: Added the in-flight refresh map and preserved the public signature.

blocks.content: The first pass is small: share pending work, await it, then remove the key.

blocks.title: src/auth/session.ts
tests
blocks.content: 17 passed · typecheck clean

events.title: Review requested
events.summary: Handed the diff to a fresh adversarial context.

blocks.content: Implementation complete. Please review concurrency safety, cleanup behavior, and API compatibility.

blocks.title: Dispatch reviewer
blocks.content: context: diff only · posture: find the way this is wrong

blocks.title: handoff
blocks.content: waiting for adversarial review

events.title: Adversarial review
events.summary: Reviewer traces rejection instead of trusting happy-path tests.

blocks.content: I’m treating the patch as incorrect until every exit path is accounted for. The success path cleans up; now I’ll trace rejection.

blocks.title: Trace
blocks.content: requestNewSession rejection → await refresh throws → function exits

blocks.title: Control flow
blocks.content: refreshes.delete(id) is unreachable when the promise rejects.

events.title: Blocking finding
events.summary: A rejected promise remains cached and poisons every later retry.

blocks.title: P1 · stale rejected promise
blocks.content: A failed refresh never leaves the map. Every later caller receives the same rejected promise, turning a transient failure into a permanent sign-out loop.

blocks.title: failure path
review
blocks.content: changes requested

events.title: Revision applied
events.summary: Moved cleanup into finally and added a retry regression test.

blocks.content: Confirmed. Cleanup must cover both resolution paths. I moved it into finally and added the missing rejection case.

blocks.title: src/auth/session.ts
Focused test
blocks.content: rejects first request → releases key → second request succeeds

events.title: Fix verified
events.summary: Both success and failure paths now release the session key.

blocks.title: Verify
blocks.content: bun test tests/auth/session.test.ts && bun run typecheck

blocks.title: Result
blocks.content: 18 tests passed · 0 failed · typecheck clean

blocks.title: review verdict
blocks.content: Concurrent callers deduplicate; failures release the key; a subsequent retry succeeds.

events.title: Approved
events.summary: The race and its failure path are covered without expanding the API.

blocks.content: The blocker is resolved. The revised patch is narrow, reversible, and covers the failure mode the first pass missed.

blocks.title: approved
blocks.content: ready to merge after CI

cases.title: The negative timestamp
cases.summary: Reviewer catches an invalid signed timestamp.

blocks.content: I’ll normalize the persisted timestamp before comparing it with the current clock.

blocks.title: src/cache/expiry.ts
blocks.content:
const expiresAt = Number(record.expiresAt);
return expiresAt <= Date.now();

events.title: Signedness is unchecked
events.summary: Reviewer finds that negative values bypass expiry.

blocks.title: Blocking
blocks.content: A negative persisted timestamp is accepted without validation and can create an invalid cache lifetime.

blocks.title: Approved
blocks.content: The parser rejects negative and out-of-range timestamps before cache comparison.

cases.title: The eager default
cases.summary: Reviewer catches fallback work executing too early.

blocks.content: I’ll use the discovered value when present and compute a fallback otherwise.

events.title: Preserve the primary path
events.summary: Claude plans a compact optional fallback.

blocks.title: Plan
blocks.content: Keep lookup first, then provide the default expression.

events.title: Initial fallback
events.summary: Claude adds an eager fallback expression.

blocks.title: src/config/load.ts
blocks.content: const config = discovered ?? buildDefaultConfig();

events.title: Default performs side effects
events.summary: Reviewer finds the default is built too early.

blocks.title: Blocking
blocks.content: The target helper evaluates both arguments, so buildDefaultConfig writes files even when discovered is present.

events.title: Lazy fallback verified
events.summary: Reviewer approves the explicit lazy branch.

blocks.title: Approved
blocks.content: The default builder now runs only when no discovered configuration exists.
